title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL sink accepts conflicting password sources
summary: The Oracle MySQL sink accepted both an inline password and a password environment variable in the same configuration.
observed: Configuration validation did not reject two competing secret sources, which made the effective source depend on implementation details.
expected: Configuration must fail closed when both password sources are configured.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for conflicting password sources.
impact: Operators could believe one secret source is active while another is actually used.
delivery_semantics: The fix is startup-only validation and does not change ACK ordering or committed-write behavior.
security: Failing closed on secret-source ambiguity supports least privilege and avoids accidental use of inline credentials.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL configuration rejects simultaneous password and password_env values.
Valid single-source password configurations continue to work.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL unit test suite.
documentation: Clarify that password and password_env are mutually exclusive in the Oracle MySQL sink documentation.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the validation fix is published.
